content:
Dear Mr. Thorne,

Thank you for your prompt response to our inquiry dated April 21, 2026, referenced under Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187. This office appreciates MoveOps Inc.'s cooperative posture and the detail provided in your initial correspondence regarding the scope of your internal documentation audit.

EXTENSION OF RESPONSE DEADLINE — GRANTED

After review of your request, this office is granting a 15-calendar-day extension of the original response deadline. The revised deadline for submission of all requested documentation is:

    June 5, 2026

This extension is granted on the understanding that MoveOps Inc. is actively compiling documentation and coordinating with its third-party motor carriers, as represented in your letter of April 21, 2026. Please note that this extension is a one-time accommodation and no further extensions will be considered absent extraordinary circumstances communicated in writing.

SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS

I want to take this opportunity to reiterate the documentation standards that will apply to your submission:

1. All shipping papers, placarding certificates, carrier certifications, and emergency response documentation must be submitted as signed originals or certified copies. Unsigned drafts, internal memoranda, or verbal attestations will not be accepted as compliant documentation.

2. For each shipment identified in your review, documentation should be organized and clearly labeled by shipment, including the date of transport, origin and destination, material description, hazard classification, and the name of the motor carrier.

3. Training records for MoveOps Inc. personnel involved in the offering of hazardous materials for transportation must include the employee's name, date of training, description of training content, and the name and qualifications of the trainer, as specified under 49 CFR 172.704(d).

4. If any documentation for a specific shipment cannot be located or was not prepared at the time of transport, MoveOps Inc. should clearly identify the gap in its submission and provide an explanation of the circumstances along with any corrective measures that have been implemented.

ADDITIONAL NOTICE

Please be advised that the quality and completeness of MoveOps Inc.'s submission will be a factor in determining whether additional compliance oversight is warranted. Incomplete submissions, or submissions that reveal systemic documentation deficiencies, may result in an expanded review of MoveOps Inc.'s carrier coordination practices and hazardous materials offering procedures, including potential on-site inspection pursuant to 49 U.S.C. 5121(c).

Conversely, a thorough and well-organized submission demonstrating good-faith compliance efforts will be noted favorably in the administrative record for this inquiry.

If you have any questions about the submission requirements or the revised deadline, please do not hesitate to contact me directly.

Respectfully,

Linda Castellano
Senior Compliance Investigator
Hazardous Materials Division
Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration
U.S. Department of Transportation
Phone: (202) 366-4488
Email: linda.castellano@dot.gov

Case Reference: PHMSA-2026-04-1187
cc: PHMSA Eastern Region Field Office
subject: RE: DOT Hazardous Materials Division — Formal Compliance Inquiry — MoveOps Inc. (Shipper of Record) — Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187

For Priya Naidu and Jonathan Marsh, we want to scope correctly upfront.

For Priya, we will handle housing outreach in a way that reflects her ADA service animal requirements rather than treating this like a standard pet-screening case. To do that cleanly, please send:
- preferred move-in date
- origin city
- whether she will need temporary housing on arrival
- any building or neighborhood preferences already discussed
- the type of service animal accommodation documentation BrightLoop plans to provide to support housing conversations

For Jonathan, since you noted a higher-touch executive experience, please send:
- expected start date in Boston
- origin city
- whether spouse/children are relocating with him
- whether he needs furnished temporary housing first or wants us to source long-term housing immediately
- preferred neighborhood(s)
- whether you want airport meet-and-greet, home setup coordination, or other white-glove services included in scope

3) International relocation
For Oliver Bancroft, we need to separate immigration timing from housing timing so we do not create a false start date on our side. Please send:
- citizenship and current work authorization status
- BrightLoop's intended visa path and outside immigration counsel, if already selected
- planned Boston start date versus earliest physically movable date
- whether household goods are shipping from London

For all six employees, it would also help if you can send a simple roster with full name, title, origin location, manager contact, target start date, and whether each move is employee-only or household-inclusive. If easier, I can send you a template today and we can work from that.

Separately, we are aligning an internal service structure so that standard, international, and executive support levels are clearly defined before we finalize scope or pricing with you. I would rather be precise now than give you a fast answer that changes later.

If you send the employee details above, I can come back with a phased rollout plan and next-step recommendations. My goal is to get you an initial operating view quickly, then tighten timelines and service tiers as we confirm the specialized pieces.

If useful, I can also make time tomorrow for a 20-minute working call to walk through the six employees one by one.

Best,
Mina
subject: Re: Six May Boston relocations + board visibility

content:
Mina, Chloe,

Carmen flagged the landlord hold on April 8 in her email about Jae-won Kim's Austin lease still pending guarantor packet. I saw it and replied the next morning that I would handle the guarantor paperwork by end of week. I did not send it.

That week got overtaken by the Deshawn Hartley trade-show deadline relocation and I treated Jae-won's file like something I could circle back to after the rush. I never did. I promised follow-up
I missed the follow-up
I then created bad completion signals in Linear and Airtable

If you want, I can help reconstruct the exact overlap with the Deshawn Hartley work so the audit trail is complete, but I don't want that to sound like an excuse. The simpler version is the accurate one: I dropped Jae-won during a rush move and the systems I updated afterward hid the miss.

We are reopening the case immediately today. Our operations team is sending the required guarantor packet to UrbanNest before close, and we are asking for confirmation from the landlord as soon as it is reviewed. The Airtable relocation record and associated Linear workflow should not have shown completed, and those records are being corrected to reflect active recovery status until the signed lease is actually in hand.

Third, Marcus reviewed the billing issue and confirmed the $500 goodwill credit was posted against the wrong document on our side. It should have been applied to StormCloud invoice INV-2026-0319 and was not. Finance is correcting that.

Fourth, Simone Richter’s standing desk reimbursement is not being questioned on legitimacy. It is currently caught in the blanket stipend hold finance put in place during the fraud review. That does not make the delay acceptable from your perspective, and I will make sure you have a clear status rather than a vague pending answer.

Fifth, on the Ridgeline introduction from Anya: I confirmed we were too slow to act on that referral. We are responding directly today so that your team is not left having vouched for us without follow-through.

I know this is landing right before your leadership review, and I also know the combined effect here is a trust problem, not just a lease problem. I’ll stay on point on this account until the housing, billing correction, stipend status, and referral follow-up are all closed with evidence.
